name: Southwest Economic Solutions Dba Miwealth
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit offering free homebuyer education, pre-purchase counseling, and financial coaching to help aspiring homeowners understand the mortgage process.
description_long:
Southwest Economic Solutions, operating under the DBA Miwealth, is a HUD-approved housing counseling agency focused on homeownership preparation. The organization employs professional, bilingual, and MSHDA & HUD-certified counselors dedicated to demystifying the home purchase process for underserved populations. Founded on the principle that financial literacy and expert guidance are critical barriers to homeownership, they have built a track record of direct impact in their community. In 2021 alone, they provided homebuyer education to over 400 families and individuals, with 100 of their counseled clients successfully purchasing homes.

Southwest Economic Solutions offers multiple pathways to homeownership readiness. Their flagship service is free monthly homebuyer education workshops held virtually on Zoom on the second Friday of each month, covering the complete mortgage qualification process with input from lenders, realtors, inspectors, and industry partners. Participants receive a certificate of completion valid for 12 months—a requirement for most lenders and all down payment assistance programs. For those seeking faster completion, they offer a self-paced online pre-purchase program through eHome America for $99. One-on-one counseling with HUD-certified counselors is also available to address individual affordability questions, loan qualification barriers, and down payment assistance options from various sources.

What distinguishes Southwest Economic Solutions is their bilingual capacity, HUD certification, and integration with established financial institutions. They list major bank sponsors including Bank of America, CIBC, Citizens, Fifth Third, Flagstar, Huntington, and US Bank, indicating strong institutional partnerships. Their My Home portal provides structured account creation and appointment scheduling, removing friction from accessing counseling. The organization's explicit focus on Detroit and their demonstrated outcomes (100 home purchases from their clientele in 2021) show measurable community impact rather than theoretical benefits.

The primary limitation is geographical scope—the website indicates focus on Detroit/Michigan homebuyers without explicitly serving nationwide populations. The paid online course option ($99) is minimal cost but creates a two-tier service. While free homebuyer education is offered monthly, availability may be constrained for those unable to attend scheduled group sessions or afford the expedited course. Like most non-profits, capacity constraints may affect counselor availability for one-on-one sessions. Their impact data ends at 2021, leaving current service capacity unclear.
pros: Completely free monthly homebuyer education workshops on Zoom, removing cost barriers for low-income buyers
HUD-approved and MSHDA-certified counselors ensure professional, standardized guidance aligned with federal requirements
Bilingual services and counselor staff explicitly serve non-English-speaking communities underrepresented in homeownership
Partnerships with major banks (Bank of America, Flagstar, Huntington, US Bank, etc.) provide direct access to lenders and industry expertise
Certificates of completion valid for 12 months satisfy lender requirements and down payment assistance program prerequisites
One-on-one counseling addresses individual barriers to qualification, including affordability assessment and down payment assistance exploration
Demonstrated track record: 100 clients from 2021 counseling cohort successfully purchased homes; 400+ received education
cons: Limited geographic scope; website and results focus exclusively on Detroit/Michigan with no clear nationwide availability
Monthly workshop schedule (second Friday only) may not accommodate working individuals or those needing flexible timing
One-on-one counseling availability not specified; non-profits often have capacity constraints and long wait times
Self-paced online course requires $99 payment, creating cost for those wanting faster completion than monthly workshops
Impact data on website ends at 2021; current service capacity, counselor availability, and outcomes remain undisclosed
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Detroit/Michigan with limited budgets who need free education and government-backed certification
Non-English speaking prospective homeowners seeking bilingual counseling and culturally competent guidance
Applicants for down payment assistance or first-time homebuyer programs that mandate homebuyer education completion
Individuals uncertain about mortgage qualification or affordability who benefit from one-on-one financial coaching
diagnosis: Southwest Economic Solutions is best for first-time homebuyers in the Detroit area—particularly those with limited incomes, non-English speakers, or applying for down payment assistance—who need free, HUD-certified education and counseling to navigate mortgage qualification. The key caveat is geographic limitation to Michigan; outside this region, the organization cannot serve you, and you should seek local HUD-approved housing counseling agencies instead.
services: Free monthly homebuyer education workshops via Zoom (second Friday of each month)
HUD-approved pre-purchase counseling and education program
Home affordability assessment and financial coaching
Down payment assistance exploration and guidance
Self-paced online pre-purchase program through eHome America ($99)
Certificate of completion for lender and down payment assistance program requirements
My Home account portal for scheduling and intake management
Home purchase process education covering lending, inspection, and realtor partnerships
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
